beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check weather and route plans for the day
beats.reasoning: Mei is organized and weather-aware; waking early on a rainy Tuesday to plan around the morning drizzle and SR-520 closure is in character.

beats.activity: Bike commute to Downtown Seattle shop, avoiding SR-520 via local streets
beats.reasoning: Mei cycles to work; the SR-520 eastbound closure doesn't affect her westbound ride from Wallingford to Downtown, but light rain means slightly slower pedaling and careful routing.

beats.activity: Work at bike shop: repair and maintenance, indoor focus due to rain
beats.reasoning: Morning shift on a steady rain day; Mei focuses on shop work rather than outdoor tasks, and the steady rain makes it a good day for interior repairs and inventory.

beats.activity: Lunch at Pike Place Market, grabbing a quick bite indoors to stay dry
beats.reasoning: Mei is familiar with Pike Place from yesterday; rainy weather makes it ideal to grab food while sheltered, and it's a short walk from the shop.
Short walk back to the shop; rain is still steady until 2pm.

beats.activity: Afternoon shift at bike shop: customer service, repairs, and restocking
beats.reasoning: Full afternoon at the shop; rain clears by 2pm, but Mei stays inside handling walk-ins and maintenance work through close of business.
Evening commute; rain has cleared, so Mei can enjoy the ride home at a relaxed pace after a full work day.

beats.activity: Dinner at home: simple meal and review transit maps
beats.reasoning: Mei has moderate energy (50/100 from yesterday); she settles in at home with a quiet dinner, continuing to organize her transit collection and weekend route planning.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: salsa dancing at a studio in Capitol Hill
beats.reasoning: Mei values close friends and salsa dancing is a core hobby; a mid-week evening class lifts her mood after a rainy work day and aligns with her organized, future-focused personality.
Drive or bus back from Capitol Hill to Wallingford after dance class; allows time to wind down before bed.

diary: Woke to that familiar grey Seattle drizzle—the kind that makes you grateful for indoor work. The SR-520 closure meant everyone was rerouting through side streets, but I pedaled through Wallingford to the shop with my eyes half-closed, just me and the rain. The shop was steady all morning; there's something meditative about fixing derailleurs when the world outside is soaked and slow. Grabbed soup at Pike Place around one—the market was packed with people ducking in from the weather. Afternoon flew by with tune-ups and a chatty customer who wanted to talk about gravel bikes for an hour. By evening I was damp and tired but not the bad kind. Changed into dry clothes and headed over to Velocity for salsa. The studio was warm and bright, and I needed that. My hips remembered the steps better than my brain did. Rode home through the wet streets, feeling loose and alive.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure shaped everything—I knew the commute would be chaos, so I stuck to local streets and just accepted the slower pace. It actually felt good, grounding. Work was naturally focused indoors anyway, which suited the day. The lunch break was practical; Pike Place kept me from going stir-crazy after being inside all morning. I almost skipped dance class because the weather made me want to cocoon, but I'm glad I didn't—that's become my Tuesday anchor, the thing that reminds me I'm not just a mechanic, I'm someone who moves and feels alive. The combination of steady work, then the physicality and joy of dancing, balanced out the grey day perfectly.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
